channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Iran War Expands to Lebanon’s Hezbollah
item.description: Israeli strikes hit a suburb of Beirut, Lebanon, on Monday in retaliation for attacks from Hezbollah, an Iran-backed militant group.
item.dc:creator: Shawn Paik

media:content.media:credit: Hussein Malla/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Smoke rising in Beirut, Lebanon, early on Monday.

item.title: Friedrich Merz Has Bigger Problems Than Trump
item.description: Friedrich Merz’s chancellorship is in choppy waters.
item.dc:creator: Anna Sauerbrey

media:content.media:credit: Jan Staiger for The New York Times

item.title: ‘Vladimir,’ Plus 7 Things to Watch on TV this Week
item.description: The new limited series starring Leo Woodall and Rachel Weisz premieres, and two true-crime documentaries air.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Gonzalez

media:content.media:credit: Courtesy of Netflix
media:content.media:description: Rachel Weisz and Leo Woodall in “Vladimir.”

item.title: Bagram Air Base Targeted by Pakistan, Afghanistan Says
item.description: The Afghan military said it had thwarted a Pakistani airstrike on the former U.S. base, Bagram airfield, amid an intensifying campaign that has targeted dozens of military sites across the country.
item.dc:creator: Elian Peltier and Safiullah Padshah

media:content.media:credit: David Guttenfelder for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Taliban fighters at the Bagram air field in Afghanistan in 2021.

item.title: No Corrections: March 2, 2026
item.description: No corrections appeared in print on Monday, March 2, 2026.

item.title: Quote of the Day: Nostalgic Young Audience Rediscovers the iPod
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Monday, March 2, 2026.

item.title: In Alysa Liu and Eileen Gu, China and America See a Mirror Image
item.description: The Olympic athletes are the subject of uncomfortable public comparisons that present online narratives that overlap more than both sides may realize.
item.dc:creator: Li Yuan

media:content.media:credit: Vincent Alban/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Ms. Liu, during a training session at the Milan ice skating arena.

item.title: China’s Parents Are Outsourcing the Homework Grind to A.I.
item.description: Parents in China are turning to A.I. chatbots and other tools to help their children gain an edge and ease the fighting over homework.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Wang and Jiawei Wang

item.title: China’s New Study Buddy: The A.I. Chatbot
item.description: Parents in China are using homegrown chatbots like DeepSeek and Doubao as well as Google’s Gemini to help their children with homework and give them an academic edge.
item.dc:creator: Jiawei Wang

item.title: Unforgettable SAG Actor Awards 2026 Looks: Teyana Taylor, Emma Stone and More
item.description: Painted torsos, tulip skirts, Jazz Age style and more.
item.dc:creator: The Styles Desk

media:content.media:credit: Tommaso Boddi/WWD, via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Emma Stone sported a pastel sweater with a spaghetti-strap dress to match.

item.title: Iran Strikes U.S. Military Facilities in Dubai, Bahrain and Other Middle East Locations
item.description: A Times analysis of satellite imagery and verified videos shows Iran found vulnerabilities in U.S. locations in the region.
item.dc:creator: Devon Lum and Haley Willis
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: U.S. Takes Casualties as Attacks on Iran Intensify
item.description: The U.S. and Israel attacked Iran for a second day, and Iran retaliated, striking Israel and Persian Gulf countries. One strike killed three American service members at a base in Kuwait.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Mitssunaga and Shawn Paik
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times

item.title: Israel Strikes Beirut After Hezbollah Attacks
item.description: Residents fled in panic as Israeli jets struck Hezbollah strongholds in response to a midnight barrage, an expansion of the war between Israel, the U.S. and Iran.
item.dc:creator: Christina Goldbaum and Ephrat Livni

media:content.media:credit: Hussein Malla/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Smoke rising in Beirut, Lebanon, early on Monday.

item.title: Democrats Denounce Trump’s Iran Attack, but Subtle Divisions Emerge
item.description: While most Democrats have opposed his decision, their responses reveal differences over Middle East policy and national security that loom over the party’s future.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Lerer and Katie Glueck
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: “We can’t allow Iran to get a nuclear weapon,” said Senator Mark Kelly, who had a more tempered opinion on President Trump’s Iran attack.

item.title: Trump Says Iran War Could Last Weeks and Gives Competing Visions of New Regime
item.description: In a brief interview, he said the country’s hardened military should simply surrender their weapons to the Iranian public.
item.dc:creator: Zolan Kanno-Youngs, David E. Sanger and Tyler Pager
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump boarding Air Force One at Palm Beach International Airport in West Palm Beach, Fla., on Sunday. He spoke to The Times in a brief phone interview.

item.title: 10-Minute Challenge: Klimt’s Woman in Gold
item.description: We’d like you to look at one piece of art for 10 minutes, uninterrupted.
item.dc:creator: Larry Buchanan

item.title: In New York, Fury, Anxiety and Joy Over the Attacks on Iran
item.description: As Mayor Zohran Mamdani assailed what he called a “catastrophic escalation” in Iran, some Iranian Americans worried about what comes next, while others celebrated.
item.dc:creator: Dodai Stewart
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Victor J. Blue for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani of New York said Americans did not support a “war of aggression” in Iran.

item.title: Screen Actors Guild’s Actor Awards Winners List: Updating Live
item.description: The vampire drama was named outstanding cast and, in an upset, its star won best lead actor. Other victors included Catherine O’Hara for “The Studio.”
item.dc:creator: Kyle Buchanan

media:content.media:credit: Mario Anzuoni/Reuters
media:content.media:description: A surprised Michael B. Jordan accepting the award for “Sinners.”

item.title: Iranian Strikes on Gulf States Reach the Hundreds
item.description: Iran has fired nearly 400 missiles and over 800 drones across the Persian Gulf in the past two days, according to government reports.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar

media:content.media:credit: Hamad I Mohammed/Reuters
media:content.media:description: A damaged building in Manama, Bahrain, on Sunday.

item.title: Trump Says He Is the ‘Least Racist’ President. But His Term Echoes a Grim Past.
item.description: Woodrow Wilson’s presidency has taken on new resonance among historians and critics of President Trump, particularly when it comes to race.
item.dc:creator: Erica L. Green

media:content.media:credit: Harris & Ewing/Library of Congress
media:content.media:description: President Woodrow Wilson and his first cabinet in 1913.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for March 2, 2026
item.description: Leslie Benson calls it the way she sees it.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Kirsch

item.title: SAG Actor Awards Red Carpet 2026: See Photos of the Stars’ Looks
item.description: Timothée Chalamet, Teyana Taylor and Chase Infiniti walked the red carpet for the Screen Actor Guild’s Actor Awards on Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: The Styles Desk

media:content.media:credit: Amy Sussman/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Timothée Chalamet, nominated for best actor in a lead role for “Marty Supreme.”

item.title: Strike on Girls’ School Kills 115, Iranian State Media Says
item.description: Videos and images verified by The New York Times showed that at least half of the school was destroyed. It was not immediately clear why the school was hit, or which country’s forces had fired at it.
item.dc:creator: Malachy Browne, Ephrat Livni and Sanam Mahoozi

item.title: La caída del ayatolá da pie a una peligrosa incertidumbre
item.description: Nadie debería llorar al ayatolá Jamení, pero Trump está creando la oportunidad para el caos y la violencia con su campaña contra Irán.
item.dc:creator: El Comité Editorial

media:content.media:credit: Vahid Salemi/Associated Press

item.title: In Turn From Iran Talks to War, U.S. Casts Doubt on Diplomacy
item.description: President Trump again deployed heavy force against a country with which he had been negotiating, continuing a pattern seen in previous attacks on Iran and Venezuela.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump, seen on Friday, has given little public explanation on why he cut off talks and attacked Iran.

item.title: What Now in Iran?
item.description: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei is dead. Whoever takes his place will shape the Middle East’s future.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times

item.title: How the Assault on Iran Unfolded
item.description: Despite warnings after an earlier wave of killings, top Iranian officials gathered in person, and Israel seized the chance to kill Iran’s supreme leader.
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger and Ronen Bergman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A rally to mourn the supreme leader, Ayatollah Khamenei in Tehran on Sunday.

item.title: Investors Brace for Stock Market’s Reaction to U.S.-Israel Attack on Iran
item.description: The war comes at a precarious time for stocks, forcing investors to weigh the fallout of a potential prolonged disruption to oil supplies and logistics.
item.dc:creator: Joe Rennison and River Akira Davis
item.category: Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Karsten Moran for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The attack on Iran has brought disruption to oil markets, shipping and supply chains.

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times

item.title: Oil Prices Jump After Iran Attack
item.description: How long prices remain high will depend on what the United States, Israel and Iran do next.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca F. Elliott
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries

media:content.media:credit: Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: An oil tanker anchored near Oman was ablaze on Sunday in this photo taken from a video.

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times

item.title: Iran’s Ballistic Missile Launchers Have Been Cut by Half, Israeli Official Says
item.description: The Israeli military says it identified a significant recent acceleration in Iran’s efforts to rebuild its missile production capabilities.
item.dc:creator: Gabby Sobelman

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A missile on display in Tehran in October.

item.title: Stakes Quickly Rise for U.S. in Iran War, With 3 Service Members Killed
item.description: The grim toll signaled that Iran was more prepared for war than the Trump administration anticipated, U.S. military officials said.
item.dc:creator: Helene Cooper and Eric Schmitt
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Smoke rose on Sunday near Erbil International Airport, which hosts U.S.-led coalition troops in Iraq’s autonomous Kurdistan region.

item.title: Strikes Destroyed Newly Built Structures at Khamenei’s Compound
item.description: Images showed new roofs and structures connecting existing buildings at the compound where Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was killed on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Samuel Granados

media:content.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: Democrats Question Trump’s Urgency to Attack Iran
item.description: Lawmakers questioned whether the United States faced an “imminent” threat from Iran, a requirement for the president to lawfully initiate military action without congressional approval.
item.dc:creator: Minho Kim

media:content.media:credit: Kent Nishimura for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The War Powers Act requires the president to consult with Congress “in every possible instance” before taking military action abroad, and it limits the unilateral use of force to 60 days.

item.title: Los iraníes salen a la calle para celebrar la muerte de Jamení
item.description: Algunos iraníes dijeron en las redes sociales que lloraban en privado la muerte del líder supremo. Pero en ciudades de todo el país estallaron muestras de exaltación.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi and Christiaan Triebert
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.description: U.S. and Israeli airstrikes on Feb. 28 caused extensive damage to a school in Minab, Iran, witness videos verified by The New York Times show. Women wailed as rescue workers searched through rubble for survivors and victims.
item.dc:creator: Cynthia Silva

item.title: Epstein’s New Mexico Ranch Gets Scrutiny at Last. It May Be Too Late.
item.description: Heinous allegations have prompted state officials to restart an inquiry, which went cold in 2019, into the convicted sex offender’s lesser-known property.
item.dc:creator: Reis Thebault and Paul Ratje

item.title: What’s Next for Iran? Big Change Seems Certain, but Direction Is Unclear.
item.description: Experts say that Iran’s clerical rulers may be too deeply entrenched for Iranians to topple them, and that the U.S. and Israeli strikes risk setting off deeper radicalization or violence.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Iranians mourning their supreme leader at a rally in Tehran.

item.title: NYPD Steps Up Security After U.S.-Israel Attacks in Iran
item.description: Officers in the Police Department’s international liaison unit, including at outposts abroad, are gathering information about potential threats to New York City.
item.dc:creator: Chelsia Rose Marcius
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Jefferson Siegel for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Rebecca Weiner, deputy commissioner of intelligence and counterterrorism for the Police Department, said the city was in a heightened threat environment.

item.title: Muere el ayatolá Alí Jamení, lider supremo de Irán
item.description: Aplastó brutalmente la disidencia interna y amplió la huella de Irán en el extranjero. Su muerte se produjo en medio de un amplio ataque de Estados Unidos e Israel a Irán.
item.dc:creator: Alan Cowell and Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: El ayatolá Alí Jamení, quien gobernó durante más de tres décadas, dio forma a la revolución islámica de Irán mucho más que el fundador de la República Islámica, el ayatolá Jomeini.

item.title: Trump lanza un mensaje a los iraníes para que tomen el control de su gobierno
item.description: El ambiguo llamamiento del presidente Trump se produce después de que socavara los medios de comunicación financiados por Estados Unidos que normalmente habrían ayudado al gobierno a llegar a la gente dentro del país.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Agency for Global Media

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Los críticos afirman que los recortes del gobierno de Trump a la radiodifusión financiada por el gobierno han perjudicado la capacidad de Estados Unidos para llegar al pueblo iraní.

item.title: How Talks Between Anthropic and the Defense Dept. Fell Apart
item.description: The Pentagon and Anthropic were close to agreeing on the use of artificial intelligence. But strong personalities, mutual dislike and a rival company unraveled a deal.
item.dc:creator: Sheera Frenkel, Cade Metz and Julian E. Barnes
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Demetrius Freeman for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: In January, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth published a memo calling on A.I. to be widely integrated across the military and for A.I. companies to offer their technology without restrictions.

item.title: With Iran, Trump and Netanyahu are Doing the Free World a Favor
item.description: American hearts should be moved by moments like this, when free nations band together to administer justice and supply hope.
item.dc:creator: Bret Stephens
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Yves Herman/Reuters

item.title: La CIA ayudó a determinar la ubicación de Jamení. Israel atacó después
item.description: El asesinato del líder supremo de Irán y de otros altos cargos iraníes se produjo tras un estrecho intercambio de información entre Estados Unidos e Israel, según personas familiarizadas con la operación.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes, Ronen Bergman, Eric Schmitt and Tyler Pager
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Airbus, vía Reuters
media:content.media:description: Una imagen de satélite muestra humo negro y graves daños en el complejo del líder supremo iraní, el ayatolá Alí Jamení, tras los ataques de Estados Unidos e Israel contra Irán, el sábado.

item.title: Trump Says He’s Open to Talks With Iran in Interview With The Atlantic
item.description: In an interview with The Atlantic, President Trump said the country’s new leaders after the death of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei “want to talk” but did not say whom he was referring to.
item.dc:creator: Zolan Kanno-Youngs and Tyler Pager
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump has kept a low profile since the United States and Israel struck Iran, except to call some individual reporters.

item.title: Maersk to Halt Some Red Sea Shipping in Sign of War’s Disruption to Global Supply Chain
item.description: Though the conflict is centered on the Persian Gulf, shipping companies fear that the Iran-backed Houthi militia in Yemen could resume attacks on vessels in the Red Sea hundreds of miles to the west.
item.dc:creator: Peter Eavis
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Martin Bernetti/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Maersk, a large shipping company, said on Sunday that it would stop sending some of its vessels through the Red Sea.

item.title: Iran Fires Cheap Drones Into Arab Countries, Wreaking Havoc
item.description: It already proved its effectiveness on the battlefields of Ukraine. Now the Iranian-made Shahed-136 kamikaze has been unleashed across the Arab Gulf.
item.dc:creator: Sanjana Varghese, Adam Rasgon and Dmitriy Khavin

item.title: Where the Strikes Leave Iranians
item.description: Iran is reeling a day after the United States and Israel carried out a large attack that killed Iran’s supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. Erika Solomon, the Iran bureau chief for The New York Times, explains how ordinary Iranians are responding to the sudden end of Khamenei’s authoritarian rule and the uncertain future that lies ahead.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon, Christina Thornell, Leila Medina, Nikolay Nikolov and June Kim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
